name: Dave Lambert
abstract: David Alden Lambert (June 19, 1917 – October 3, 1966) was an American jazz lyricist, singer, and an originator of vocalese. He was best known as a member of the trio Lambert, Hendricks & Ross.

concerts.label: Horizons in Jazz featuring Dizzy Gillespie, George Shearing, and Others
Bel Canto Foundation Jazz Concert to Benefit Musicians
Midnight Performance: Thanksgiving Eve Jazz Festival
Benefit Concert for the Student Non-Violent Coordinating Committee

about_quotes.speaker: Annie Ross
about_quotes.evidence: She loved Lambert, Hendricks, and Ross... Jon and Dave walked in... Dave Lambert said, 'Wait a minute. I have an idea. Let's just the three of us record'... We did that. That’s – one of the greatest moments in my life was standing in front of the speaker and hearing the basic track that we recorded
about_quotes.doc_title: Annie Ross — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Jon Hendricks
about_quotes.evidence: Even when he criticized Lambert, Hendricks, and Ross for the Duke Ellington album... I moved in with Dave after we did Four Brothers, and we began to plan Sing a Song of Basie, the album.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jon Hendricks — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Annie Ross
about_quotes.evidence: I happened to meet Dave Lambert, who introduced me to Jon Hendricks, and that’s how “Sing a Song of Basie” came about.
about_quotes.doc_title: Annie Ross interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 13, 2001

about_quotes.speaker: Bill Crow
about_quotes.evidence: I was very lucky to have fallen in with Dave Lambert and his wife... they taught me how to survive... I really got my New York education from Davey. He showed me how to scuffle and he showed me who the interesting people were... And also through him... they all introduced me to people like Neal Heft
about_quotes.doc_title: Bill Crow interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, October 18, 1995

about_quotes.speaker: Budd Johnson
about_quotes.evidence: Dave Lambert came up with the idea, and I had to formulate it and everything, but he got the credit for the tune but it's my arrangement on the tune called What's This.

about_quotes.speaker: Jackie Cain and Roy Kral
about_quotes.evidence: And then eventually as we went along, suddenly we heard, let’s see Buddy Stewart and Dave Lambert with Gene Krupa. We had heard it around that time.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jackie Cain and Roy Kral interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, March 22, 1998
